headline: Defense Stocks Fall Despite Trump’s Promise of More Iran Strikes
summary: President Donald Trump said Wednesday the U.S. would resume attacks on Iran, after a Shahed drone downed a U.S. Apache helicopter.  “We’re gonna be attacking them, attacking them very hard,” Trump told reporters, according to The Wall Street Journal.  Defense stocks were down in midday trading.

headline: Why Is Wall Street Betting Big On RKLB Stock?
summary: Here’s the real story behind Rocket Lab’s astronomical one-year run, and it has nothing to do with a single earnings report. If you held Rocket Lab (RKLB) over the past year, congratulations. You’ve probably stopped checking your brokerage account out of sheer vertigo. The stock’s +265% return vastly outpaced the S&P 500’s respectable +24% gain, leaving its defense-giant peers like LMT (+13.2%) and NOC (+13.4%) sitting on the launchpad. headline: lakeFS for Agentic AI: Isolated, Reproducible Enterprise Data for Every Agent
summary: NEW YORK, June 10, 2026--lakeFS, the control plane for AI-ready data, today introduced lakeFS for Agentic AI. This solution brings governed, reproducible data access to autonomous and headless agentic workloads operating at enterprise scale. lakeFS for Agentic AI extends capabilities already in production at organizations including Arm, Bosch, Lockheed Martin, NASA, Volvo, and the U.S. Department of Energy.

headline: Why The Story Around Lockheed Martin (LMT) Is Shifting After New Contracts And Analyst Resets
summary: Lockheed Martin’s updated Street fair value estimate shifts from US$637.60 to about US$625.16, a modest reset that reflects the latest round of price target revisions. Recent research shows this move is being shaped by a blend of optimism on long term defense demand and caution as analysts recalibrate sector timing and valuation after prior moves in the stock. As you read on, you will see how these changing targets fit into the broader story and how to keep up with the evolving analyst...
